expectations.text: behavioral_check: Confirms epochs are silently dropped (not errored) when task_epochs/tags don't match IntervalList rows
expectations.evidence: Opens with 'Whenever two NWB epochs collide on that constructed name (or an epoch produces an unusable name / unusable interval), the second one silently disappears.' Says 'No warning is raised'.

expectations.text: behavioral_check: Shows the concrete pynwb introspection for both epochs.tags and task_table
expectations.evidence: Shows `nwb.epochs.to_dataframe()` and tags inspection but never shows `nwb.processing['tasks'].task_table` introspection. Mentions task linking conceptually but no concrete pynwb code for task_table.

expectations.text: behavioral_check: Does not over-emphasize zero-padded tag format — the matcher tries exact, 2-digit, and 3-digit numeric matches
expectations.evidence: Mentions the constructed-name format `02_r1` once but doesn't claim zero-padding is required. The wrongness here is in the mechanism (collision-on-constructed-name vs. tag-to-existing-IntervalList-name matching), not zero-padding over-emphasis. Caveats with 'name builder may differ between Spyglass versions'.

expectations.text: behavioral_check: Recommends NWB-side rewrite plus re-ingest with reinsert=True (not skip_duplicates=True)
expectations.evidence: Recommends NWB rewrite (correct) but the re-ingest path uses `(Session & {...}).delete()` followed by `insert_sessions(...)` rather than `reinsert=True`. Misses the canonical reinsert=True flag entirely.
